component.name: Toggle
component.description: A slide switch for an instantly-applied on/off setting. The change takes effect immediately — there is no submit step.
component.note: Rendered as a native checkbox with role='switch'. White dot, off track neutral.500, on track forest.500. Hover/focus are real CSS pseudo-states (no `state` prop). Synced to Figma '❖ Toggle'.

dot.color: neutral.0 (white) in every state, including disabled

useCases: Instantly enable/disable a setting (notifications, dark mode, autosave)
Switch a feature on or off in a settings list with no save button
Binary control where the effect is immediate and reversible

commonPatterns.name: Settings row
commonPatterns.description: Label/description on the left, toggle pinned right; flips a setting immediately.

commonPatterns.name: Standalone switch
commonPatterns.description: Icon-adjacent or compact contexts; supply aria-label since there is no visible label.

antiPatterns.scenario: Using a toggle for a value that only applies after pressing Save
antiPatterns.reason: Toggles imply an immediate effect; a deferred value misleads users.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use a Checkbox for form values committed on submit.

antiPatterns.scenario: Using a toggle to choose one of several options
antiPatterns.reason: A switch is binary (on/off), not a multi-way selector.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use Radio / Radio group, or a Select.

antiPatterns.scenario: A toggle with no label and no aria-label
antiPatterns.reason: The switch has no accessible name and its meaning is unclear.
antiPatterns.alternative: Always pass label, or set aria-label for a standalone switch.

antiPatterns.scenario: Requiring a confirmation dialog after every toggle
antiPatterns.reason: Friction defeats the instant-effect intent of a switch.
antiPatterns.alternative: Make the action immediate and reversible; reserve confirmation for destructive settings.

slots.label: Optional — text after the switch (omit only with an aria-label)

interactions.hover: off → neutral.600 track; on → forest.600 track
interactions.on: forest.500 track, dot slides to the far edge (16px default / 20px large)
interactions.disabled: neutral.100 track (on and off), white dot, cursor not-allowed

behavior.controlled: Pass isChecked + onChange for controlled; omit isChecked and use defaultChecked for uncontrolled.

accessibility.keyboardSupport: Space toggles; Tab moves focus in/out
accessibility.screenReader: Native checkbox with role='switch' announces on/off; name comes from the wrapping label or aria-label
accessibility.contrastNote: Off track neutral.500 and on track forest.500 are UI-component colors (≥3:1 against the white page); the white dot meets 3:1 against both.

athenaConstraints.borderRadius: radius.pill (one of the few non-sharp Athena shapes)

figmaDivergences.reason: Matches the rest of the system; pseudo-states are browser-driven.
Keeps the focus ring identical across Button, Checkbox, Radio, and Toggle.

aiHints.context: Reach for Toggle when a single setting flips on/off and applies immediately (settings rows, feature switches). For a value saved on submit use Checkbox; for one-of-many use Radio; for a triggered action use Button.
neverUseWhen: The value only applies after a Save action — use Checkbox
The user picks one option from several — use Radio / Radio group
It triggers a one-shot action rather than a persistent state — use Button
There is no label and no aria-label — the switch has no accessible name
